question_text:
Given that the greatest common divisor, gcd is expressed as:
\(g.c.d(p,q)=\left\{\begin{array}{ c c }
p & \text{if } q=0 \\
g.c.d\left(q,remainder\left(\frac{p}{q}\right)\right) & \text{if} p \ge q \text{and} q=0
\end{array}\right.\) Compute g.c.d(48,12) (4Marks)
question_latex:
Given that the greatest common divisor, gcd is expressed as:

g.c.d\left(q,remainder\left(\frac{p}{q}\right)\right) & \text{if} p \ge q \text{and} q=0
\end{array}\right.\) Compute g.c.d(48,12) (4Marks)

question_latex:
Given that the greatest common divisor, gcd is expressed as:
$$gcd(p,q) = \left\{\begin{array}{ c c }
p & \text{\textit{if q = 0}} \\
\gcd\left(q,remainder\left(\frac{p}{q}\right)\right) & \text{\textit{if p}} \\
\gcd\left(q,remainder\left(\frac{p}{q}\right)\right) & \text{\textit{if q}} \\
\end{array}\right.$$Calculate explicitly (4Marks)
